2606.28249.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
2606.28249.paper_summary: Recently, Large Language Model (LLM)-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models have achieved remarkable naturalness. However, the standard Supervised Fine-Tuning paradigm often converges to statistically averaged prosody, limiting emotional expressiveness. While preference-driven optimization offers a promising alternative, existing approaches suffer from two structural mismatches: information conflict, where content and emotion in a shared latent space produce conflicting gradients, leading to reward hacking and semantic degradation; and scale gap, where sparse sentence-level rewards struggle to guide dense frame-level generation. To overcome these challenges, we propose HPRO, a hierarchical progressive reward optimization framework. Within HPRO, we introduce the HD-Emo codec as a novel differentiable reward model to resolve the information conflict. It extracts speech into distinct content and style preference tokens, structurally isolating emotional optimization from semantic content. Building upon this structured preference space, HPRO bridges the scale gap by progressively aligning frame-, word- and sentence-level objectives. Experiments demonstrate that HPRO significantly enhances emotional expressiveness, while effectively preserving linguistic intelligibility. The code and audio samples are publicly available at https://xxh333.github.io/hpro-demo/.
2606.28249.paper_summary_zh: 最近，基於大型語言模型（LLM）的文本到語音（TTS）模型已達到顯著的自然度。<lb/>然而，標準的監督微調範式通常會收斂到統計平均的韻律，限制了情感的表達。<lb/>雖然基於偏好的優化提供了一個有希望的替代方案，但現有的方法存在兩個結構性不匹配：信息衝突，即在共享潛在空間中內容和情感產生衝突的梯度，導致獎勵黑客和語義退化；以及規模差距，即稀疏的句子級獎勵難以指導密集的幀級生成。<lb/>為了克服這些挑戰，我們提出了HPRO，一個分層漸進的獎勵優化框架。<lb/>在HPRO中，我們引入了HD-Emo編解碼器，作為一種新穎的可微分獎勵模型，以解決信息衝突。<lb/>它將語音提取為不同的內容和風格偏好標記，結構上將情感優化與語義內容隔離。<lb/>基於這一結構化的偏好空間，HPRO通過逐步對齊幀、詞和句子級目標來彌補規模差距。<lb/>實驗表明，HPRO顯著增強了情感表達，同時有效保持了語言的可理解性。<lb/>代碼和音頻樣本可在 https://xxh333.github.io/hpro-demo/ 上公開獲取。
2606.28249.author: Sihang Nie et.al.
2606.28249.authors: Sihang Nie, Xiaofen Xing, Rui Xing, Haoming Li, Ruitong Xiao, Jingyuan Xing, Baiji Liu, Xiangmin Xu
